In all usage examples, when `krealloc` fails (returns NULL), the code immediately returns -ENOMEM without freeing the original allocation. This leaves the original memory unreleased, creating a memory leak. Callers must explicitly free the original allocation with `kfree` when `krealloc` returns NULL to prevent this resource leak.
